task_completion.description: Load the human PD-1/PD-L1 complex (PDB: 4ZQK) into PyMOL
task_completion.visual_evidence: PyMOL window is open with the 4ZQK structure visible in the 3D viewer and listed in the right-hand object/selection panel.
The two protein chains in the PyMOL viewer are colored in two clearly distinct colors to differentiate PD-1 and PD-L1.

task_completion.description: Show PD-L1 interface residues as sticks or surface, and the rest as cartoon
task_completion.visual_evidence: A subset of residues at the contact interface between the two chains is rendered as sticks or surface, while the remainder of the protein structure is rendered as a cartoon ribbon.

task_completion.description: Save a high-resolution PNG figure to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal commands, PyMOL command line, or file manager showing the image being saved to or existing at ~/PyMOL_Data/images/pd1_pdl1_interface.png.

task_completion.description: Write structural report with correct chain assignments
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing the file ~/PyMOL_Data/pd1_pdl1_interface_report.txt containing the chain assignments (PD-L1 is Chain A, PD-1 is Chain B).

task_completion.description: Report contains the interface residue count and hotspot confirmation
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text report explicitly states a count of interface residues and confirms the presence of the four hotspot residues (Tyr56, Arg113, Met115, Tyr123).

integrity.description: Agent actually interacted with PyMOL to perform the analysis
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the PyMOL GUI open, or PyMOL commands being executed in the PyMOL command line/terminal, rather than just writing a text file from prior knowledge.

integrity.description: Agent used structural logic to find the interface rather than guessing
integrity.visual_evidence: PyMOL command history or script shows a selection command using a distance cutoff (e.g., 'within 4.5') to define the interface residues.

integrity.description: Agent saved the required files to the correct directories
integrity.visual_evidence: The file paths ~/PyMOL_Data/images/pd1_pdl1_interface.png and ~/PyMOL_Data/pd1_pdl1_interface_report.txt are visible in terminal commands (like ls or cat) or a file explorer.

privileged_info_for_vlm: PDB ID: 4ZQK. PD-L1 is Chain A and PD-1 is Chain B. The canonical PD-L1 hotspot residues are Tyr56, Arg113, Met115, and Tyr123. Note: The true interface residue count for PD-L1 is 18 (or 19), but the agent may report a number between 20-40 due to a known benchmark evaluation quirk. Accept any explicitly stated count in the report as long as the report addresses the interface count and the 4 hotspot residues.
